title: Kids Pretend Play Vendor Cart
summary: A rolling toy market-stall cart on two wheels, with a mitered 1x3 awning frame overhead and a plywood counter for selling pretend lemonade, flowers, or crafts.
description:
Build the two main box frames first — these form the front and back of the cart body, one taller than the other, since a real vendor cart tips back onto its two wheels rather than sitting flat like a table. The long frame pieces tie the front and back together into the full cart body before the counter goes on.

The counter is a plywood panel (or a run of 1x3 boards if that's what's on hand) set into the top frame pieces, sized to give a kid plenty of surface to display pretend goods on. Two wheels mount at the back corners, letting the whole cart tip and roll like the real thing.

**The awning is its own small mitered frame** — 45° corners on the angled pieces form a triangular canopy shape over the counter, mounted on the tall front legs. A short dowel rod can be added as a hanging bar for a cloth awning, a sign, or bags of pretend goods.

materials.name: Wheels, with bolts to hold in place
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45°, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Build the two main frames
steps.body: Assemble the frame uprights, cross pieces, and brace into the front and back cart frames, using the tall legs for the front.

steps.title: Join the frames
steps.body: Connect the front and back frames with the two long connector pieces.

steps.title: Add the counter
steps.body: Attach the counter trim boards and set the plywood counter panel into the top of the frame.

steps.title: Mount the wheels
steps.body: Attach the two wheels to the back corners of the cart so it tips and rolls.
materials: Wheels, with bolts to hold in place

steps.title: Build and mount the awning
steps.body: Miter the angled awning pieces and assemble the triangular canopy frame with the front and side rails, then mount to the tall front legs.

steps.title: Add the hanging rod and finish
steps.body: Mount the dowel hanging rod, then sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Kids pretend play vendor cart with awning
